gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وسنده الثاني مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/344)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وسنده الثاني مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/344)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ جَعَلَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ نَذْراً إِنْ قَضَى اللَّهُ حَاجَتَهُ أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقَ بِدَرَاهِمَ فَقَضَى اللَّهُ حَاجَتَهُ فَصَيَّرَ الدَّرَاهِمَ ذَهَباً وَ وَجَّهَهَا إِلَيْكَ أَ يَجُوزُ ذَلِكَ أَوْ يُعِيدُ فَقَالَ يُعِيدُ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from Ali Bin Mahziyar who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘A man made a vow upon himself, if Allah<sup>azwj</sup> were to Fulfill his need, that he would give in charity with (certain) Dirhams. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Fulfilled his need. So he turned the Dirhams as gold and has diverted these to you<sup>asws</sup>. Is that allowed, or should he repeat?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should repeat’.
en.sarwar: 11. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ali ibn Mahziyar from who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has made a vow and commitment that if Allah makes his wish to come true, he will pay a certain amount of dirham as charity. Allah then made his wish come true. He then made the dirham into gold and has sent to you; if that is sufficient or he has to do it again. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must do it again.’”
